modelPath: C:\Users\RRoberson\OneDrive - MSNPath\Documents\Dashboard Drafts\CoL Training\Big + Bulky Co Model.pbix

findings.message: Column 'Name' exists in both 'Customer' and 'Store' tables. This creates ambiguity for Copilot when users ask about 'name' without specifying which table.
findings.recommendation: Rename to 'Customer Name' and 'Store Name' respectively to disambiguate. Per Power BI Standards: column names should be human-readable and unambiguous.

findings.message: Column 'Country' exists in both 'Customer' and 'Store' tables. Copilot cannot distinguish which 'Country' a user means.
findings.recommendation: Rename to 'Customer Country' and 'Store Country' respectively, or hide duplicate geography columns from the AI schema.

findings.message: Column 'State' exists in both 'Customer' and 'Store' tables. This creates ambiguity for natural language queries.
findings.recommendation: Rename to 'Customer State' and 'Store State' respectively, or hide duplicate geography columns from the AI schema.

findings.message: Column 'Unit Price' exists in both 'Fact - Sales' and 'Product' tables. Users asking about 'unit price' will get ambiguous results.
findings.recommendation: Since fact tables should be hidden per organizational standards, hide 'Fact - Sales'[Unit Price] and keep 'Product'[Unit Price] visible. Alternatively, rename to 'Sales Unit Price' and 'Product List Price'.

findings.message: Column 'Unit Cost' exists in both 'Fact - Sales' and 'Product' tables.
findings.recommendation: Hide 'Fact - Sales'[Unit Cost] (fact table should be hidden per org standards) and keep 'Product'[Unit Cost] visible. Or rename to distinguish transaction cost from standard cost.

findings.message: Fact table 'Fact - Sales' is not hidden. Per Power BI Standards (Data Modeling > Fact Tables): 'Fact tables should be hidden from the users.' Only degenerate dimensions should be exposed.
findings.recommendation: Hide the 'Fact - Sales' table. Expose Order Number and Currency Code as degenerate dimensions if needed, but all other fields should be accessed only through DAX measures.

findings.message: Surrogate key 'CustomerKey' on dimension table 'Customer' should be hidden. Per Power BI Standards (Data Modeling > Dimension Tables): 'Hide the surrogate key from the user.'
findings.recommendation: Hide column 'CustomerKey' on table 'Customer'. It is only needed for relationships.

findings.message: Surrogate key 'ProductKey' on dimension table 'Product' should be hidden. Per Power BI Standards (Data Modeling > Dimension Tables): 'Hide the surrogate key from the user.'
findings.recommendation: Hide column 'ProductKey' on table 'Product'. It is only needed for relationships.

findings.message: Surrogate key 'StoreKey' on dimension table 'Store' should be hidden. Per Power BI Standards (Data Modeling > Dimension Tables): 'Hide the surrogate key from the user.'
findings.recommendation: Hide column 'StoreKey' on table 'Store'. It is only needed for relationships.

findings.message: No display folders detected on dimension tables. Per Power BI Standards (Data Modeling > Dimension Tables): 'Group columns in display folders to make it easier for the user to find what they are looking for.'
findings.recommendation: Create display folders to organize columns logically. For example, Customer table: 'Demographics' (Gender, Age, Birthday), 'Geography' (City, State, Country, Zip Code, Continent), 'Identity' (CustomerKey, Name).

findings.message: Measures are not organized into display folders within the measure table. Per Power BI Standards (DAX): 'Group measures in display folders to make it easier for the user to find what they are looking for.'
findings.recommendation: Create display folders such as 'Revenue', 'Cost', 'Margins' to organize measures by business area.

findings.message: Key business columns likely have no synonyms configured (not detectable from PBIX binary). Synonyms improve Copilot's natural language matching. Columns like Customer Name, Product Name, Category, and Store Name benefit from synonyms.
findings.recommendation: Add synonyms for key business columns. For example: Customer[Name] -> 'customer name, buyer, purchaser'; Product[Category] -> 'product category, type, group'; Store[Name] -> 'store name, location, shop'.

findings.message: Geography columns (City, State, Zip Code, Country, Continent) likely do not have Data Category set (not detectable from PBIX binary; verify in Power BI Desktop). Data categories help Copilot and map visuals.
findings.recommendation: Set Data Category for: Customer[City] -> City, Customer[State] -> StateOrProvince, Customer[Zip Code] -> PostalCode, Customer[Country] -> Country, Customer[Continent] -> Continent. Apply similarly to Store geography columns.

findings.message: Column 'Exchange Rate' uses Float data type. Per Power BI Standards (Data Modeling > General): 'Avoid float data types unless necessary.' Exchange rates may be acceptable as float, but consider Decimal for precision.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate whether Decimal Number type would be more appropriate for Exchange Rate to avoid floating point precision issues in calculations.

findings.message: Column 'Weight' uses Float data type. Per Power BI Standards: 'Avoid float data types unless necessary.'
findings.recommendation: Consider whether Decimal Number type would be more appropriate for Weight values.

findings.message: Columns like Year, Month Number, CustomerKey, ProductKey, StoreKey, Order Number, Line Number, Age, Store Code, Subcategory Code, Category Code should have default summarization set to 'Don't Summarize'. Per Power BI Standards (DAX): 'Do not use default aggregations -- explicitly define summary aggregations as measures.'
findings.recommendation: Set 'Don't Summarize' on all ID, key, code, year, and number columns that should not be aggregated. This prevents accidental SUM of Year or Key columns.

findings.message: Text columns like Month, Month Short, Day of Week, Day of Week Short, Quarter, Year Month, and Year Month Short should have Sort By Column configured to sort in chronological order rather than alphabetically. Per Power BI Standards (Data Modeling > Dimension Tables): 'Sort text columns based on relevant numerical columns.'
findings.recommendation: Configure Sort By Column: Month -> Month Number, Month Short -> Month Number, Day of Week -> Day of Week Number, Day of Week Short -> Day of Week Number, Year Month -> Year Month Number, Year Month Short -> Year Month Number, Quarter -> Year Quarter Number.

findings.message: No relationship exists between 'Fact - Sales'[Order Date] and 'Date'[Date]. Only 'Delivery Date' is connected to the Date table. Order Date is likely the primary date for most analyses but has no relationship to the Date dimension.
findings.recommendation: Create a relationship from 'Fact - Sales'[Order Date] to 'Date'[Date]. Per Power BI Standards (Data Modeling > Dates): 'If possible use one date table if there are multiple date fields and use the function USERELATIONSHIP instead of creating additional date tables.' Make Order Date the active relationship and Delivery Date inactive, then use USERELATIONSHIP in measures that need Delivery Date context.

findings.message: The Date table is likely not marked as a date table (Time Intelligence is disabled: __PBI_TimeIntelligenceEnabled = 0). Per Power BI Standards (Data Modeling > Dates): 'Mark calendar table as date table.'
findings.recommendation: Mark the Date table as a date table in Power BI Desktop (Table Tools > Mark as Date Table). Also ensure auto-generated date tables are turned off per standards.

findings.message: Measure 'Total Cost' uses SUM('Fact - Sales'[Unit Cost]) which sums unit costs without multiplying by quantity. This likely produces incorrect results. Compare with 'Total Revenue' which correctly uses SUMX with quantity multiplication.
findings.recommendation: Change to: SUMX('Fact - Sales', 'Fact - Sales'[Unit Cost] * 'Fact - Sales'[Quantity]) to calculate the actual total cost accounting for quantity purchased.

findings.message: Measure 'Total Sales' uses COUNT('Fact - Sales'[Order Number]) which counts the number of order lines, not a monetary total. The name 'Total Sales' implies a monetary sum but the calculation returns a count.
findings.recommendation: Either rename to 'Total Order Lines' or 'Order Count', or change the calculation to compute a monetary sales total (e.g., SUMX of quantity * price). If both metrics are needed, create separate measures with clear names.

findings.message: Measure 'Gross Margin' ([Total Revenue] - [Total Cost]) depends on 'Total Cost' which appears to have a logic error (sums unit cost without quantity). This cascading error affects the Gross Margin calculation.
findings.recommendation: Fix the 'Total Cost' measure first, then verify Gross Margin produces correct results.

findings.message: The model has a Date table but no time intelligence measures (TOTALYTD, SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR, DATEADD, etc.). Time Intelligence is explicitly disabled (__PBI_TimeIntelligenceEnabled = 0). This limits Copilot's ability to answer time-comparison questions.
findings.recommendation: Create time intelligence measures such as YTD Revenue (TOTALYTD), Previous Year Revenue (CALCULATE + SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR), and YoY Growth %. Per Power BI Standards: 'Use a date table for time intelligence calculations.'

findings.message: Measure 'Gross Margin' references [Total Revenue] and [Total Cost] without table qualification. Per Power BI Standards (DAX): 'Never use table names (not fully qualified) for measures.' While this works, it is a standards violation.
findings.recommendation: Change to: '⍟ Big & Bulky Measures'[Total Revenue] - '⍟ Big & Bulky Measures'[Total Cost]. Note: The org standard is nuanced -- it says not to use unqualified references, so fully qualify measure references.

findings.message: The model has only 4 business measures (Total Revenue, Total Cost, Total Sales, Gross Margin) for a sales model. Common metrics like Average Order Value, Customer Count, Revenue per Customer, Quantity Sold, etc. are missing. Copilot performs better with a rich set of pre-defined measures.
findings.recommendation: Add measures for common business questions: Average Order Value, Distinct Customer Count, Quantity Sold, Revenue per Store, Revenue per Product Category, etc. Per Power BI Standards: 'Do not use default aggregations -- explicitly define summary aggregations as measures.'

findings.message: Measures 'Total Sales' and 'Total Revenue' have similar names but different calculations. 'Total Sales' counts order lines while 'Total Revenue' sums monetary value. This naming creates confusion for business users and Copilot.
findings.recommendation: Rename 'Total Sales' to 'Order Line Count' or 'Transaction Count' to clearly distinguish from the monetary 'Total Revenue' measure.

findings.message: No AI schema (Copilot/schema.json) detected. This file controls which fields are visible to Copilot and configures synonyms. Without it, Copilot will see all fields including keys, codes, and noise columns.
findings.recommendation: Configure the AI schema in Power BI Desktop: Model view > Copilot pane > select/deselect fields. Hide key columns, code columns, and intermediate fields. This is a critical step for 'Prep for AI'.

findings.message: No AI instructions (Copilot/Instructions/instructions.md) detected. AI instructions provide critical business context that Copilot uses for DAX generation. Without them, Copilot has no understanding of business terminology or calculation preferences.
findings.recommendation: Create AI instructions that define: (1) Business context (Big & Bulky Co sells large/bulky products), (2) Key metrics and how they should be calculated, (3) Default time periods, (4) Business terminology, (5) Preferred grouping dimensions.

findings.message: No verified answers (Copilot/VerifiedAnswers/) detected. Verified answers provide pre-built, tested responses to common business questions, improving Copilot reliability.
findings.recommendation: Create verified answers for common questions: 'What is total revenue?', 'Show sales by product category', 'What are the top 10 customers?'. Each should have 5-7 trigger phrases covering formal and conversational variations.

findings.message: The 'Prepped for AI' flag is not enabled on this model. This flag signals to Copilot that the model has been reviewed and optimized for AI-assisted analysis.
findings.recommendation: Enable the 'Prepped for AI' flag in Power BI Desktop model settings after completing all other AI preparation steps.

findings.message: ID, key, and code columns (18 columns) are likely visible to AI. These include CustomerKey, ProductKey, StoreKey, Country Code, State Code, Product Code, Subcategory Code, Category Code, Store Code, Line Number, and sort-helper number columns. These create noise for Copilot.
findings.recommendation: Exclude from AI schema: all surrogate keys (CustomerKey, ProductKey, StoreKey), all code columns (Country Code, State Code, Product Code, Subcategory Code, Category Code, Store Code), sort-helper number columns (Month Number, Day of Week Number, etc.), and Line Number.

findings.message: No Row-Level Security (RLS) roles are defined. Per Power BI Standards (Security and Sharing): 'Define at least two RLS roles: Admin and General.'
findings.recommendation: Create at minimum an Admin role (no filters) and a General role (with appropriate filters applied). Use AAD groups for role membership.

findings.message: The Date table Power Query is filtered to only Q1-2015 ("Filtered Rows" = Table.SelectRows(dbo_Date, each ([Year Quarter] = "Q1-2015"))). This severely limits the model's date range and breaks any analysis outside Q1-2015. Sales data may span a wider date range.
findings.recommendation: Remove or expand the date filter to cover the full range of dates in the Fact - Sales table. A date table should span at minimum from the earliest to the latest date across all fact tables.
